Rv MEIvr\
C H A
R MA S
FAIR
POLITICAL
PRACTICES Co1MISs1ON
February 20,
1996
J.
William Zastrow
Member,
Board of Directors,
Division 5
Central Basin Municipal Water District
17761 Regency Circle
Beliflower,
California
90706
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—96-027
Dear Mr.
Zastrow:
You have requested advice regarding the conflict—of—interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”)
1 as they
pertain to your position as a member of the Board of Directors of
the Central Basin Municipal Water District.
Pursuant to Section 83114(b)
and Commission Regulation 18329,
the Commission provides formal written advice and informal
assistance to public officials concerning their duties under the
Act.
However, it has been the Commission’s long—standing policy
not to provide written advice or telephonic assistance to one of
the parties to pending litigation.
(Alperin Advice Letter,
No.
1—86—ill;
and Woodside Advice Letter,
No.
A—85—106,
Regulation 18329(b) (8) (C).)
At the present time,
you and your business, Peerless Water
Company,
are named defendants in the lawsuit of City of Vernon v.
Central Basin Water District.
The conflict—of-interest issues
pending in the lawsuit are the same issues for which you have
requested advice.
The Commission believes that once a matter
concerning a public official’s duties under the Act is pending
before a court,
the determination of the law’s application is in
the hands of the judicial branch.
i
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18995.
428 J STREET. SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please contact me at 916/322-5660.
Sincerely,
Jil6’t)
Sta f Counsel,
Legal Division
JS:ak

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply to Mr. Zastrow's position as a Board member of the Central Basin Municipal Water District.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the conflict-of-interest issues are currently pending in litigation (City of Vernon v. Central Basin Water District), and the Commission's policy is not to provide advice to parties in pending litigation where the same legal issues are at stake. The determination of the Act's application is appropriately in the hands of the judicial branch.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter citing the Commission's long-standing policy against providing advice to parties in pending litigation. The document contains no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections but does present the underlying request and the basis for declination. References to prior advice letters (Alperin and Woodside) support the policy cited.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply to Mr. Zastrow's position as a Board member of the Central Basin Municipal Water District.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the conflict-of-interest issues are currently pending in litigation (City of Vernon v. Central Basin Water District), and the Commission's policy is not to provide advice to parties in pending litigation where the same legal issues are at stake. The determination of the Act's application is appropriately in the hands of the judicial branch.

William Zastrow Member, Board of Directors, Division 5 Central Basin Municipal Water District 17761 Regency Circle Beliflower, California 90706 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A—96-027 Dear Mr. Zastrow: You have requested advice regarding the conflict—of—interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) 1 as they pertain to your position as a member of the Board of Directors of the Central Basin Municipal Water District. Pursuant to Section 83114(b) and Commission Regulation 18329, the Commission provides formal written advice and informal assistance to public officials concerning their duties under the Act. However, it has been the Commission’s long—standing policy not to provide written advice or telephonic assistance to one of the parties to pending litigation. (Alperin Advice Letter, No. 1—86—ill; and Woodside Advice Letter, No. A—85—106, Regulation 18329(b) (8) (C).) At the present time, you and your business, Peerless Water Company, are named defendants in the lawsuit of City of Vernon v. Central Basin Water District. The conflict—of-interest issues pending in the lawsuit are the same issues for which you have requested advice. The Commission believes that once a matter concerning a public official’s duties under the Act is pending before a court, the determination of the law’s application is in the hands of the judicial branch. i Government Code Sections 81000—91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000—18995. 428 J STREET. embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide formal written advice to Mr. Zastrow regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act because the same issues are pending in current litigation involving him and his business as defendants.
